questions.q: Who were Bhishma's parents?
options: Pandu and Kunti
Parashara and Satyavati
Pratipa and Sunanda
Shantanu and the goddess Ganga
questions.explanation: Bhishma was born to King Shantanu of Hastinapura and the river goddess Ganga as the only child she did not return to the river.

questions.q: What was Bhishma's birth name?
options: Devadatta
Devavrata
Devapi
Devasena
questions.explanation: He was named Devavrata at birth and earned the name Bhishma only after swearing his terrible oath.

questions.q: Which of the eight Vasus was Bhishma's incarnation?
options: The eighth, cursed to a long life among mortals
The first, leader of the celestial brothers
The fourth, keeper of the sacred fire
The sixth, lord of the rushing winds
questions.explanation: Bhishma was the eighth Vasu, Dyaus, whose greater guilt condemned him to a long earthly life while his brothers were freed at birth.

questions.q: What did Bhishma famously vow for life?
options: Never to refuse a request from a brahmin
Never to retreat from any battlefield
Celibacy and renunciation of the throne
Silence on every full-moon day
questions.explanation: Bhishma swore lifelong celibacy and gave up his claim to Hastinapura so his father could marry Satyavati.

questions.q: What rare power did Bhishma's father grant him?
options: Sight of all events anywhere on earth
Death only at the hour of his own choosing
Strength of ten thousand elephants
Command over the waters of the Ganga
questions.explanation: In gratitude for the great sacrifice, Shantanu granted Bhishma the boon of dying only when he himself willed it.

questions.q: Which army did Bhishma command at Kurukshetra?
options: The Panchala host of Drupada
The Pandava army of seven akshauhinis
The Yadava forces from Dwarka
The Kaurava army of Duryodhana
questions.explanation: Bound by duty to Hastinapura's throne, Bhishma served as the first commander of Duryodhana's Kaurava army.

questions.q: For how many days did Bhishma lead the Kaurava forces?
options: Ten
Eighteen
Thirteen
Fifteen
questions.explanation: Bhishma commanded for the first ten days of the eighteen-day war, falling on the tenth.

questions.q: How was Bhishma finally brought down from his chariot?
options: Bhima struck him down in a mace duel
Drona withdrew his protection at dusk
Arjuna's arrows, shot from behind Shikhandi, pierced him
Krishna hurled his discus on the tenth day
questions.explanation: Because Bhishma would not fight Shikhandi, Arjuna shot from behind him until the grandsire fell onto a bed of arrows.

questions.q: By what affectionate title is Bhishma known among the Kurus?
options: The Regent of the River
The Lion of Hastinapura
The Grandsire, or Pitamaha
The Terrible Archer
questions.explanation: Both Pandavas and Kauravas revered Bhishma as Pitamaha, the grandsire of the entire Kuru house.

questions.q: How was Bhishma's thirst quenched as he lay on the bed of arrows?
options: Arjuna pierced the earth and a spring of pure water rose to his lips
Krishna summoned rain clouds to cool and refresh him
Duryodhana brought sweet water in a golden vessel
Ganga herself overflowed her banks to reach her son
questions.explanation: When Bhishma asked for water, Arjuna shot an arrow into the earth and a spring, often said to be Ganga's own water, rose to his mouth.

questions.q: From whom did Bhishma learn the science of weapons?
options: Drona, master of the Kuru princes
Parashurama, the axe-bearing scourge of kings
Kripa, the immortal teacher
Indra, lord of the heavens
questions.explanation: Bhishma studied the art of war under Parashurama, the same master who would later become his opponent.

questions.q: What dispute drove guru and student, Parashurama and Bhishma, to fight?
options: Bhishma's refusal to surrender the Vijaya bow
Bhishma's abduction of brides by force from Kashi
Bhishma's refusal to marry the rejected princess Amba
Bhishma's defiance of a summons to the hermitage
questions.explanation: Parashurama took up Amba's cause and ordered Bhishma to marry her, and when Bhishma refused because of his vow, they fought.

questions.q: How long did the duel between Bhishma and Parashurama last?
options: Three days, ending in Bhishma's surrender
Eighteen days, like the great war itself
Seven days, ending at the gods' command
Twenty-three days, with neither overcome
questions.explanation: The duel raged for twenty-three days without a victor before the gods and ancestors intervened to end it.

questions.q: Whose three daughters did Bhishma carry off from a swayamvara?
options: The king of Kashi's: Amba, Ambika, and Ambalika
The king of Madra's: Madri and her sisters
The king of Panchala's: Draupadi and her kin
The king of Gandhara's: Gandhari and her sisters
questions.explanation: Bhishma abducted the three princesses of Kashi from their swayamvara, defeating all the assembled kings.

questions.q: For whose marriage did Bhishma abduct the three princesses?
options: His nephew Pandu
His half-brother Vichitravirya
His half-brother Chitrangada
His ward Dhritarashtra
questions.explanation: Barred from marriage himself, Bhishma won the brides for his young half-brother Vichitravirya, the king of Hastinapura.

questions.q: Why did Bhishma refuse to raise weapons against Shikhandi?
options: Shikhandi had once saved his life in battle
A curse forbade him to harm any Panchala
Shikhandi carried no weapon of equal rank
He would not fight one who had been born a woman
questions.explanation: Bhishma's code forbade him to fight anyone born a woman, and he knew Shikhandi was Amba reborn as Drupada's daughter.

questions.q: When the fallen Bhishma asked for a pillow fit for a warrior, what did Arjuna give him?
options: Three arrows to cradle his head
His own folded battle standard
A cushion of kusha grass
The Gandiva bow laid beneath him
questions.explanation: Rejecting the soft pillows the kings brought, Bhishma accepted three arrows from Arjuna as the only rest worthy of a warrior.

questions.q: Why did Bhishma delay his death for weeks after falling in battle?
options: He hoped to see Duryodhana crowned emperor first
He was bound to live until the war's last day
He waited for the sun's northern course, Uttarayana
He could not die while Ganga's waters flowed
questions.explanation: Using his father's boon, Bhishma held death at bay until the auspicious Uttarayana, when the sun turns north.

questions.q: What did Bhishma deliver from the bed of arrows after the war ended?
options: His confession of the sins of the Kuru court
The plans for rebuilding shattered Hastinapura
A curse upon Ashwatthama for the night raid
Long teachings on dharma and kingship to Yudhishthira
questions.explanation: In the Shanti and Anushasana Parvas, the dying Bhishma instructed Yudhishthira at length on dharma, statecraft, and duty.

questions.q: After Shantanu died, in what role did Bhishma guide Hastinapura?
options: As crowned king for a span of ten years
As regent and guardian for his young half-brothers
As chief priest conducting the royal rites
As ambassador to the kingdoms of the south
questions.explanation: True to his vow, Bhishma never took the crown but governed as guardian for Chitrangada and Vichitravirya and the kings after them.

questions.q: What daily toll did Bhishma promise Duryodhana he would take in the war?
options: Ten thousand warriors slain each day
One Pandava brother captured each day
An akshauhini destroyed each day
One enemy commander beheaded each day
questions.explanation: Bhishma pledged to slay ten thousand warriors every day of his command, a promise he grimly kept.

questions.q: What limit did Bhishma set on his service to Duryodhana's cause?
options: He would fight only until Drona was ready to command
He would never battle his own guru Parashurama
He would not slay any of the five Pandava brothers
He would lay down arms if Karna entered the field
questions.explanation: Bhishma told Duryodhana plainly that he would fight the Pandava armies but would never kill the five brothers he loved.

questions.q: What deed earned the Vasu reborn as Bhishma his long human life?
options: He mocked the sage Vasishtha's austerities
He refused to bow before the goddess Ganga
He stole the sage Vasishtha's cow Nandini to please his wife
He spilled the soma reserved for the gods
questions.explanation: The Vasu Dyaus stole Vasishtha's wish-granting cow Nandini at his wife's urging, so the sage condemned him to a long mortal life.

questions.q: How did Krishna nearly break his own vow of not fighting because of Bhishma?
options: He leapt from the chariot and charged at Bhishma himself when Arjuna fought too gently
He strung the Gandiva himself to answer Bhishma's arrows
He sent the Yadava reserves to storm Bhishma's position
He cursed Bhishma so that his celestial weapons would fail
questions.explanation: Enraged that Arjuna would not strike his grandsire with full force, Krishna leapt down and charged at Bhishma — discus raised in the epic's telling, a chariot wheel in popular retellings — while Bhishma joyfully welcomed death at his hands.

questions.q: Who told the Pandavas the means by which Bhishma could be brought down?
options: Vidura, in a riddle sent through a messenger
Krishna, who had divined it in meditation
Ganga, appearing to Yudhishthira in a dream
Bhishma himself, when they visited his tent by night
questions.explanation: On the ninth night the Pandavas went to Bhishma's own tent, and he counselled them to place Shikhandi before Arjuna.

questions.q: What device marked Bhishma's battle standard?
options: A white conch on a field of red
A golden palm tree and five stars
A silver crane against the moon
A black river coiling round a spear
questions.explanation: Bhishma's banner bore a golden palmyra palm and five stars, a landmark of the Kaurava lines for ten days.

questions.q: When Draupadi asked the dice-hall whether a man who had lost himself could stake her, how did Bhishma answer?
options: He commanded the game annulled at once
He declared that she had been fairly won
He left the hall rather than reply
He said the course of dharma was too subtle to decide
questions.explanation: Bhishma confessed that dharma is subtle and he could not resolve her question, a silence that haunted him ever after.

questions.q: How many nights does Bhishma say he lay upon the bed of arrows?
options: Fifty-eight
Eighteen
One hundred and one
Thirty-six
questions.explanation: In the Anushasana Parva, Bhishma states he has lain fifty-eight nights on the points of the arrows while awaiting Uttarayana.

questions.q: Which warrior of Virata's house fell to Bhishma on the war's first day?
options: Uttara, the young prince of the chariots
Shveta, the prince who raged at his brother's death
Shankha, the middle son of the king
Kichaka, the marshal of the Matsya host
questions.explanation: Bhishma slew Virata's son Shveta, who had stormed the Kaurava lines to avenge his brother Uttara, killed earlier that day by Shalya.

questions.q: Which celebrated hymn did Bhishma utter in Krishna's presence before dying?
options: The Purusha Sukta of the Veda
The Anu Gita of the later books
The Vishnu Sahasranama, the thousand names
The Gajendra Moksha of the Purana
questions.explanation: From the bed of arrows Bhishma recited the Vishnu Sahasranama, the thousand names of Vishnu, in praise of Krishna.
